data.title: reviewer-findings.json not in .gitignore -> accidentally committed by agent worktrees, causes merge conflicts
data.description:
## Incident Overview
reviewer-findings.json (written by code-reviewer sub-agents to the repo working directory) is not listed in .gitignore. During epic 615f-fad3, multiple agent worktrees (ae49f130, ae5419fe, a6ec5a0f, ad186ba2) each committed this file, causing content merge conflicts when merging those worktrees into the session branch.

## Expected Behavior
reviewer-findings.json should be ignored by git (it is a transient review artifact, analogous to .checkpoint-needs-review and other runtime sentinels already in .gitignore). It should never appear in git status, never be committed, and never cause merge conflicts.

## Actual Behavior
The file accumulates in the working tree root after reviewer sub-agents run, gets picked up by 'git add .' or staged accidentally, and is then committed. When multiple worktrees each commit different versions, merging them produces CONFLICT (content) errors on reviewer-findings.json.

## Fix
Add reviewer-findings.json to .gitignore (one-liner fix). Related: 04ca-4c91 tracks the underlying ARTIFACTS_DIR mismatch that causes the file to land in the working tree in the first place.
